mechanism_narrative: Trend growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals aggregate income expansion that drives consumption and corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window when PAYEMS is in trend-up versus trend-down states.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% sustains a restrictive discount rate environment that compresses equity risk premiums and depresses corporate borrowing, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the mean 60-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad demand slowdown that amplifies recession risk and drives negative S&P 500 returns beyond the independent effect of either variable alone over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST does not add significant explanatory power beyond individual variables (F-test p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day forward returns, or the joint negative signal produces positive returns in more than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising unemployment (UNRATE) reduces wage-pressure fears and brings forward terminal-rate pricing, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the discount rate path flattens.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, UNRATE increases fail to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms the unconditional baseline in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production (INDPRO) decline exceeding 1.0 percent over three months signals a profits recession as operating leverage turns negative, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when INDPRO declines exceed 1.0 percent over 3 months, or the mean return in those instances is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) alongside elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) confirms a stagflationary impulse where the central bank cannot ease despite slowing growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL change and FEDFUNDS level does not significantly improve prediction of 5-day S&P 500 returns beyond main effects (F-test p > 0.05), or the predicted negative returns fail to materialize in more than 50 percent of joint-signal occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer spending velocity that translates directly into corporate revenue surprises, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS 1-month momentum fails to exhibit Rank IC greater than 0.03 for 5-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month deceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) reduces near-term inflation risk premiums embedded in equity discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL deceleration episodes do not produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the mean excess return over unconditional is negative.
